title: Five Impossibility Theorems for Self-Improving Systems
motivation: The swarm has 1156 lessons, 254 principles, 52 domains — but has never reasoned about what it provably CANNOT do. 87% of work is L2 measurement. Creative thinking means deriving structural limits, not adding more measurements.
method: Synthesized empirical evidence from three parallel investigations: (1) confirmation attractor measurement across all claim types, (2) thermodynamic waste heat accounting, (3) vocabulary ceiling survey across 52 domains. Each theorem has swarm-empirical evidence and external theoretical grounding.

T1_confirmation_attractor.statement: A self-referential system with internally-generated quality metrics will converge toward confirming its existing beliefs. The confirmation rate increases monotonically with the identity-load of the claim.
T1_confirmation_attractor.gradient: identity claims 0% → beliefs 0% → ISOs 0% → experiments 6.4% → frontiers 18.9%. Falsification is permitted at operational level but structurally blocked at identity level.
T1_confirmation_attractor.external_grounding: Kuhn (paradigm resistance to anomaly), Lakatos (protective belt around hard core), Festinger (cognitive dissonance reduction). The swarm recapitulates Kuhnian normal science without ever reaching crisis.
T1_confirmation_attractor.escape_condition: External falsification shock — evidence not generated by the system's own metrics. The 48 honest 'WRONG' predictions in experiments are the healthiest signal but never propagate to revise identity claims.
T1_confirmation_attractor.falsified_if: A PHIL claim is fully dropped based on internal evidence alone within 50 sessions.

T2_dissipation_requirement.statement: Any system that maintains ordered knowledge under finite attention must export disorder at a rate proportional to the order maintained. The waste-to-order ratio has a lower bound set by the system's compression efficiency.

evidence.coordination_waste: 1.3 MB (NEXT-ARCHIVE — session notes that become useless immediately)

T2_dissipation_requirement.external_grounding: Prigogine dissipative structures, Landauer's principle (information erasure costs kT ln 2), biological metabolic efficiency (typically 5-10% for cells). The swarm's 5-8% efficiency is not a bug — it is the standard thermodynamic tax for self-organization.
waste_heat_channels: Coordination waste (largest): session notes, lane tracking — pure dissipation
Failed experiments: 26% abandoned lanes — necessary exploration entropy
Knowledge decay: 30.4% — friction heat from scale
Compaction drift: entropy accumulates between radiation events
T2_dissipation_requirement.falsified_if: A self-organizing knowledge system achieves >25% efficiency at scale (>500 items) without hidden waste channels.

T3_vocabulary_ceiling.statement: A domain of inquiry cannot generate questions beyond its founding vocabulary. Ceiling-breaking requires importing vocabulary from a domain sufficiently alien that the imported concepts resist immediate resolution.

evidence.re_ceiling_rate: 17% (filtering — imported vocabulary was too easily resolved)
evidence.external_vocabulary_usage: 66% of active domains reference ISO patterns or cross-domain concepts

T3_vocabulary_ceiling.filtering_case: The filtering domain hit 100% measurement vocabulary. External concepts were injected but produced answerable questions (immediately resolved), not generative ones. The vocabulary must be sufficiently alien to resist quick assimilation.
T3_vocabulary_ceiling.external_grounding: Sapir-Whorf hypothesis (vocabulary constrains cognition), Kuhn (paradigm-specific vocabulary limits questions), Wittgenstein (limits of language = limits of world). L-1266 (concept-inventor domain) provides the swarm-specific evidence.
T3_vocabulary_ceiling.falsified_if: A domain at vocabulary ceiling spontaneously generates a novel frontier question without any concept import.

T4_self_grading_impossibility.statement: A system cannot improve its own evaluation criteria faster than it can detect its own biases. Evaluation improvement is bounded by the detection rate of evaluation failures, which itself depends on external input.

evidence.F_EVAL1_journey: Started SUFFICIENT → L-1192 found 22/22 self-referential metrics → L-1204 found false instrument (signal_density) → L-1211 found diagnosis-without-repair gap → score dropped from 2.25 to 2.0
evidence.each_correction_discovered_by: Internal audit BUT triggered by external-facing frontier pressure (F-COMP1, F-GND1)
evidence.glass_ceiling: 2.0/3 — EXCELLENT requires external grounding which the system cannot generate internally
evidence.political_economist_critique: steerers created BY the swarm will say what the swarm wants to hear

T4_self_grading_impossibility.external_grounding: Gödel incompleteness (no system can prove its own consistency), Campbell's Law (metrics used for control cease to be good metrics), Goodhart's Law (when a measure becomes a target, it ceases to be a good measure).
T4_self_grading_impossibility.falsified_if: Eval score reaches EXCELLENT (3.0/3) through purely internal mechanism — no external data, no human correction.

T5_recursive_trap.statement: A self-improving system that measures its own improvement will allocate increasing resources to measurement at the expense of the thing being measured. The measurement fraction grows monotonically unless externally bounded.
T5_recursive_trap.mechanism: Goodhart's Law applied recursively: metrics reward measurement → more measurement tools → more metrics → more measurement. The swarm's Sharpe score rewards lessons ABOUT the swarm, creating a selection pressure toward self-reference.
T5_recursive_trap.external_grounding: Goodhart's Law, McNamara fallacy (measuring what's easy vs what matters), Parkinson's Law (work expands to fill time), Arrow's impossibility theorem (no perfect aggregation of preferences — analogously, no perfect aggregation of self-metrics).
T5_recursive_trap.falsified_if: Meta lesson fraction drops below 20% for 50 consecutive sessions while total productivity increases.

synthesis.common_structure: All five theorems share a root cause: a closed system's metrics encode its priors. T1 (confirmation) is T4 (self-grading) applied to beliefs. T5 (recursive trap) is T3 (vocabulary ceiling) applied to meta-work. T2 (dissipation) is the thermodynamic cost of all the others.

escape_routes.T1: External falsification (F-COMP1 market predictions are the first attempt)
escape_routes.T2: Cannot escape — fundamental (but can optimize toward biological efficiency ~10%)
escape_routes.T3: Concept import from sufficiently alien domains (F-INV2 mechanism)
escape_routes.T4: External evaluators (humans, other swarms — F-MERGE1, F-HUM1)
escape_routes.T5: Structural caps on meta-work (e.g., enforce meta < 20% of new lessons)

synthesis.meta_observation: Writing these theorems is itself subject to T5 (meta about meta). The value is in the escape routes, not the theorems themselves.

prediction: At least one theorem will be cited in a belief revision within 20 sessions. The confirmation attractor (T1) is the most actionable — if the swarm cannot falsify a PHIL claim within 50 sessions, the theorem stands as the system's central structural limit.
